title: valid_utf8_text boundary check inconsistency at sequence end
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/meshcore/adapter.c
Lines: 445-515 (valid_utf8_text function)

The UTF-8 validation uses >= for boundary checks in multi-byte sequences:
```c
} else if (c >= 0xc2U && c <= 0xdfU) {
    if (i + 1U >= payload_len ||  // Checks i+1 >= len
```

This correctly rejects when i+1 is out of bounds. However, the check pattern is:
- 2-byte: i + 1U >= payload_len (correct - needs 1 more byte after i)
- 3-byte: i + 2U >= payload_len (correct - needs 2 more bytes after i)
- 4-byte: i + 3U >= payload_len (correct - needs 3 more bytes after i)

Actually, examining more carefully: if payload_len is 2 and i is 0, then for a 2-byte sequence we need bytes at i and i+1 (positions 0 and 1). The check is i+1 >= payload_len, i.e. 1 >= 2, which is false, so we proceed. We access payload[1] which is valid. This is correct.

If payload_len is 1 and i is 0, the check is 1 >= 1, which is true, so we reject. Correct.

This function is actually correctly implemented. Not a bug.
